title: Early-life inhalant allergen exposure, filaggrin genotype, and the development of sensitization from infancy to adolescence.
ehr_evidence: The study uses a population-based birth cohort with direct measurements (dust samples, genotyping) rather than EHR or linked health data.
summary: Longitudinal study investigating how filaggrin (FLG) mutations modify the effect of early-life inhalant allergen exposure on allergic sensitization in a birth cohort.
key_findings: Significant interaction between FLG and Fel d 1 exposure on cat sensitization (greater effect in children with FLG mutations).
Mite sensitization risk with Der p 1 exposure was consistently greater in children with FLG mutations.
Significant interaction between FLG and dog ownership, with lower risk of sensitization in children with FLG mutations exposed to dogs in infancy.
exposure_domain: inhalant allergens (house dust mite, cat, dog)
limitations: Not specified in the provided text
